message.content: Adversaries may disable or modify cloud firewalls to bypass security controls and gain unauthorized access to cloud resources. Unlike system firewalls, cloud firewalls use security groups and rules to restrict network activity to trusted IP addresses and specific ports/protocols. By introducing new firewall rules or policies, adversaries can create ingress rules that permit any TCP/IP connectivity, facilitating command and control (C2) communications, lateral movement, and data exfiltration. Common exploitation methods include using scripts or utilities to alter firewall settings. Mitigation strategies involve implementing strict access controls, regularly auditing firewall configurations, and employing automated monitoring to detect and respond to unauthorized changes in real-time.
